title: calochlaena novae-guineae
intro: Calochlaena novae-guineae, also known as the New Guinea fern, is a type of fern native to the tropical regions of Australia and New Guinea. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Calochlaena novae-guineae are said to include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and alleviating digestive problems, as well as possessing antiseptic and antiviral properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the plant are typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are also used in poultices and ointments to treat various health conditions.
